steps.title: Use intercepts to shrink the choices
steps.content: The line of best fit crosses the vertical axis a little above $16{,}000$ when $t=0$. That eliminates choices B and D immediately, because their intercepts are about $4{,}690$ and $9{,}060$. Only A and C have a reasonable intercept.

steps.title: Use a two-point regression for the slope
steps.content: For a calculator check, put two readable points from the line into Desmos, such as $(0,16000)$ and $(5,40000)$, then run $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m\approx 4800$ and $b\approx 16000$, so the slope should be near $4{,}800$, not near $9{,}060$.

steps.title: Match the equation
steps.content: Choice A, $n=16{,}090+4{,}680t$, has the right intercept and a slope close to the fitted slope. Choice C has the right intercept but a slope almost twice as large. Therefore choice A is correct.
